output.summary: Proven is a financial incentive-based habit app that pays users daily for consistency. Users lock small stakes, prove daily progress with uploads, and earn money for completion. The product targets productivity-focused professionals and students seeking accountability through financial motivation.

drafts.title: I put $50 on my morning routine. Here's what happened.
drafts.hook: I put $50 on my morning routine. Here's what happened.
drafts.body:
For 3 months, I tried building a consistent morning routine. Failed every time.

Then I tried something different: I put money on the line.

$50 stake. Upload proof daily. Earn back $2 every day I complete it.

The psychology shift was instant:
→ Loss aversion kicked in (I hate losing money)
→ Daily rewards felt tangible (not just streaks)
→ Proof uploads created real accountability

Result: 28/30 days completed last month.

The difference? Financial stakes turn habits from 'nice to have' into 'must do.'

Willpower fades. Money talks.
drafts.cta: What habit would you put money on? 💰

drafts.title: Why proof uploads beat self-reporting
drafts.hook: Why proof uploads beat self-reporting for habits (thread) 🧵
drafts.body:
1/ Self-reporting habit apps have a 90% failure rate

Why? Because lying to yourself is too easy.

2/ Proven requires photo/video proof for every habit completion

No proof = no payment

3/ This creates two accountability layers:
→ Financial (you lose money)
→ Social (community sees your proof)

4/ Result: Users complete 85% more habits vs traditional apps

5/ The magic isn't the money

It's the inability to lie to yourself when proof is required
drafts.cta: Ready to try proof-based habits? Join a challenge →

drafts.title: Your First Financial Accountability Challenge
drafts.hook: Welcome to Proven - where consistency pays
drafts.body:
Hi [Name],

You joined our waitlist because traditional habit apps weren't working.

Here's why Proven is different:

💰 You lock a small stake ($10-50)
📸 Upload daily proof of completion
💵 Earn money back each day you show up

Our most successful challenges:
→ Morning walk (5 min)
→ Phone off by 10:30 PM
→ Clean workspace daily
→ 5-minute meditation

Small habits. Real money. Actual results.

Ready to put your money where your habits are?

drafts.title: The Complete Guide to Financial Habit Accountability
drafts.hook: Why putting money on your habits changes everything
drafts.body:
Traditional habit apps fail because they rely on motivation and streaks. But motivation fades, and broken streaks feel devastating.

Financial accountability works because it taps into loss aversion - the psychological principle that losing $10 feels worse than gaining $10 feels good.

Here's how to implement financial accountability for any habit:

1. Choose a meaningful stake amount
2. Set clear, measurable completion criteria
3. Require daily proof of completion
4. Create automatic financial consequences
5. Build in daily rewards for consistency

The key is making the financial stakes significant enough to matter, but small enough to be sustainable. Most successful users stake $20-50 per habit.

When money is on the line, excuses disappear and consistency emerges.
drafts.cta: Ready to try financial accountability? Start your first challenge.

experiments.name: Proof Upload Social Sharing
experiments.hypothesis: Users who share their daily proof uploads on social media will have higher completion rates and drive organic acquisition
experiments.successMetric: 20% increase in habit completion rate for users who share proof publicly

experiments.name: Team Challenge Format
experiments.hypothesis: Remote teams using Proven for collective habits (clean workspace, daily standup prep) will have higher engagement than individual users
experiments.successMetric: 30% higher monthly retention for team challenge participants

experiments.name: Micro-Stakes Testing
experiments.hypothesis: Lower stakes ($5-10) with higher frequency rewards will drive more sign-ups than current $20-50 stakes
experiments.successMetric: 25% increase in challenge join rate with micro-stakes option
